subject: science
title: Grade 4 Science
description: Electricity and circuits, food chains, and the digestive system.

lessons.title: Electricity and Circuits
lessons.objective: Understand how simple electrical circuits work.
lessons.content:
Electricity is a form of ENERGY that powers devices in our lives.

A simple CIRCUIT needs:
  🔋 A BATTERY (power source)
  💡 A BULB or BUZZER (component)
  🔌 WIRES (to connect everything)
  🔲 A SWITCH (to turn on and off)

For electricity to flow, the circuit must be COMPLETE — a closed loop!
If there's a gap (like an open switch), electricity can't flow.

🔧 CONDUCTORS let electricity flow through them:
  • Metals (copper, iron, aluminium)
  • Water (that's why we don't mix electricity and water!)

🛑 INSULATORS block electricity:
  • Plastic, rubber, wood, glass
  • That's why wire coatings are made of plastic!

exercises.question: What must a circuit be for electricity to flow?
exercises.hint: Think about what happens when you break a circuit — does the bulb stay on?
exercises.explanation: A circuit must be a complete, closed loop with no gaps for electricity to flow through it.

exercises.question: Which of these is an electrical conductor?
exercises.hint: Metals are good at letting electricity pass through them.
exercises.explanation: Copper is a metal and an excellent electrical conductor. That's why most wires have copper inside!

lessons.title: Food Chains
lessons.objective: Understand how energy passes through a food chain.
lessons.content:
A FOOD CHAIN shows how energy moves from one living thing to another.

☀️ Sun → 🌿 Grass → 🐰 Rabbit → 🦊 Fox

Each part has a name:
  🌿 PRODUCER — makes its own food using sunlight (plants)
  🐰 PRIMARY CONSUMER — eats plants (herbivore)
  🦊 SECONDARY CONSUMER — eats herbivores (carnivore)
  🦁 TOP PREDATOR — nothing hunts it!

Arrows show the direction energy flows (→ means 'is eaten by').

If one part of the food chain is removed, it affects everything:
  • No grass? Rabbits have nothing to eat
  • No rabbits? Foxes have nothing to eat

exercises.question: In a food chain, what is a 'producer'?
options: A plant that makes its own food
exercises.correctAnswer: A plant that makes its own food
exercises.explanation: Producers are plants that make their own food through photosynthesis using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide.

exercises.question: In the food chain: Grass → Rabbit → Fox, which animal is the primary consumer? (one word)
exercises.hint: The primary consumer is the first animal that eats the producer.
exercises.explanation: The rabbit is the primary consumer because it directly eats the producer (grass).
